manifest.name: Lantern

name: Lantern

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
Patch: type `moment` locally so the plugin audit stops flagging it.

The community-plugin audit lints **without installing dependencies**, so Obsidian's `moment` re-export (`export const moment: typeof import('moment')`) can't resolve ‚Äî it becomes an error type that propagates through every date call, tripping `@typescript-eslint/no-unsafe-*` across the daily-note code. (The `moment` devDependency added in 1.6.1 couldn't help: the audit never runs `npm install`.)

Fix: `src/search/dailyNotes.ts` now casts Obsidian's `moment` to a narrow local `MomentFactory` / `MomentInstance` type, so the date code is type-safe whether or not the `moment` module resolves. Verified by reproducing the audit env (`mv node_modules/moment` away): the raw pattern fires all four warnings; the cast ‚Üí 0. Removed the ineffective `moment` devDependency.

Types-only ‚Äî `moment` still comes from Obsidian's bundle at runtime. `main.js` unchanged.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
Tooling patch ‚Äî cut so the store re-validates.

Declares `moment` as a direct devDependency. Obsidian's `moment` re-export was only a *transitive* dep of obsidian, so a clean install typed it as `any` ‚Äî tripping `@typescript-eslint/no-unsafe-*` across the daily-note code (`resolveDateWord` / `.format()` / `.clone()` / `.subtract()`). Pinning `moment@2.29.4` (Obsidian's own version) as a direct devDependency resolves the types.

Types-only: `moment` comes from the `obsidian` re-export and stays external, so `main.js` is byte-identical to 1.6.1. No behavior change ‚Äî this release exists only to trigger store re-validation.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
Patch: tool names now encode scope, so a small local model stops mis-routing reads.

- **`read_file` ‚Üí `read_vault`, `web_search` ‚Üí `search_web`.** Every searcher is now `search_<scope>`; every reader names its scope. The old `search_vault` ‚Üí `read_file` mismatch made models call the vault reader on `search_references` hits (reference-collection docs) ‚Üí "no file found".
- **`read_vault` misses now redirect** to `read_reference` when reference collections are enabled.
- **Dispatch alias map** (`read_file` ‚Üí `read_vault`, `web_search` ‚Üí `search_web`) keeps stale saved prompt overrides routing ‚Äî not exposed to the model.
- Internals renamed to match (`searchWeb` / `searchPerplexity` / `searchExa` / ‚Ä¶). Exa's `web_search_exa` MCP method left as-is (their contract).

No config or data changes. Obsidian 1.7.2+.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
**llama.cpp server feature parity** ‚Äî four recent llama-server capabilities, wired into the grounded chat agent.

- **Parallel tool calls** ‚Äî read-only tools requested in one turn run concurrently; a batch containing a write tool stays sequential. A `search_vault` + `search_references` + `read_file` turn is now one round trip, not three.
- **Perf + cache readout** ‚Äî the per-answer footer reports generation speed and KV prompt-cache reuse ‚Äî `‚Ä¶ ¬∑ 41 tok/s ¬∑ 1.2k cached` ‚Äî read from `timings.predicted_per_second` and `usage.prompt_tokens_details.cached_tokens`.
- **Answer now** ‚Äî a persistent button that ends an over-thinking model's reasoning mid-stream (`reasoning_control` ‚Üí `POST /v1/chat/completions/control`), then stops tool-calling and forces the answer. No-ops gracefully where the endpoint is absent.
- **Cold-start awareness** ‚Äî reads the router's `/v1/models` `status.value`; a sleeping/loading model shows "Waking the model (cold start‚Ä¶)" instead of a blank wait, and the settings model picker shows each model's load state.

Still read-only out of the box. Needs the [qmd](https://github.com/tobi/qmd) CLI plus a local OpenAI-compatible LLM (llama-server / LM Studio). Obsidian 1.7.2+.
